title: Init offers MCP install scope choice: project-local vs global
description: Add --mcp-install=<global|local> flag to the init command (default: global, matching current behavior). When 'local' is selected, the MCP stage in init: (1) detects the project's package manager via lockfile presence, (2) installs @fenglimg/fabric-server as a devDependency, (3) writes client configs pointing to the project-local node_modules path. Extend createServerEntry() in writer.ts to accept an optional serverPath override. Add a test file covering both modes with mocked package installs.

files.change: Add 'mcp-install' string arg with choices global|local and default 'global'; wire into the MCP config stage: if mcpInstall==='local', detect package manager, run install command, resolve local server path, pass to writers.
Update createServerEntry(serverPath: string) to accept an optional override (the function already takes serverPath; verify signature matches and add JSDoc noting local vs global usage).
Extract resolveServerPath() so it can accept an explicit localPath override; when localPath is provided, skip import.meta.resolve and use the provided path directly.
Add cli.init.mcp.install.global, cli.init.mcp.install.local, cli.init.mcp.install.prompt, cli.init.mcp.local.installing, cli.init.mcp.local.installed keys.
Mirror all new MCP install scope keys added to en.ts.

New test file: (a) global mode writes absolute server path from import.meta.resolve into client config; (b) local mode calls pnpm add -D with mocked execa, then writes project-relative path; (c) default is global.

implementation: Add 'mcp-install' arg to citty args schema in init.ts: { type: 'string', description: t('cli.init.mcp.install.prompt'), default: 'global' }.
Add a detectPackageManager(cwd: string): 'pnpm' | 'npm' | 'yarn' helper function in init.ts or a new utility file: checks for pnpm-lock.yaml, yarn.lock, package-lock.json in cwd; defaults to npm.

In the MCP stage block (added in IMPL-004): branch on args['mcp-install']. If 'local': (a) call detectPackageManager, (b) spawn the install command ('pnpm add -D @fenglimg/fabric-server' or 'npm install --save-dev ...'), (c) resolve the local path as path.join(cwd, 'node_modules/@fenglimg/fabric-server/dist/index.js'), (d) pass this localPath to createServerEntry().
If 'global' (default): use existing resolveServerPath() behavior unchanged — import.meta.resolve('@fenglimg/fabric-server').
Add i18n keys to both locale files for the new install scope UI strings.

Create packages/cli/__tests__/init-mcp-scope.test.ts: use vi.mock to mock the child process spawn for local install; assert that config written differs between global and local modes; verify default is global.

reference.pattern: detectPackageManager via lockfile presence; conditional branch in MCP stage; createServerEntry already accepts serverPath param
reference.examples: exploration-integration-points.json (global_vs_local_install): 'no current mechanism to detect local vs global; FAB_SERVER_PATH is only escape hatch'; writer.ts createServerEntry L22-27 — existing signature to extend

anchor_context: exploration-integration-points.json (GAP-4) — stale path risk; local mode uses 'node' from PATH preferred

criteria: 'fabric init --mcp-install=global' (or omitting the flag) writes the same absolute server path as today's 'fabric config install' — no behavioral change
'fabric init --mcp-install=local' adds @fenglimg/fabric-server to devDependencies in target project package.json
'fabric init --mcp-install=local' writes a project-relative server path (node_modules/@fenglimg/fabric-server/dist/index.js) to client configs
Default mcp-install is 'global' so existing workflows are unaffected
Both test cases in init-mcp-scope.test.ts pass

rationale.chosen_approach: --mcp-install=<global|local> flag with global as default preserves backward compatibility while exposing local mode
alternatives_considered: Auto-detect based on whether fab runs from global or local .bin — no reliable detection mechanism exists per exploration
Separate 'config install --local' subcommand — user said init is the sole entrypoint
decision_factors: User clarified (Round 2): 'Init offers install-scope choice via flag or prompt'
Default global preserves current behavior for all existing users
Flag approach is explicit and testable
rationale.tradeoffs: User must know to pass --mcp-install=local; no auto-detection; acceptable given user clarification

manual_checks: fabric init --mcp-install=global: .claude/settings.json mcpServers.fabric.args[0] is an absolute global path
fabric init --mcp-install=local: package.json devDeps includes @fenglimg/fabric-server; .claude/settings.json args[0] is relative node_modules path
success_metrics: init-mcp-scope.test.ts: both global and local mode tests pass
No regression on default (global) behavior in integration test
unit: init-mcp-scope: global mode writes absolute path
init-mcp-scope: local mode installs devDep and writes relative path
